nodes.name: 南冰洋
nodes.summary: 南冰洋是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括大賊鷗、Snowy albatross、Sooty albatross等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 麦夸里岛
nodes.summary: 麦夸里岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Snowy albatross、Antarctic prion，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 凯尔盖朗群岛
nodes.summary: 凯尔盖朗群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联4种鸟类，包括Snowy albatross、Grey-backed storm petrel、Antarctic prion等4种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 污染
nodes.summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

nodes.name: 南島 (新西蘭)
nodes.summary: 南島 (新西蘭)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括白眶几维、New Zealand grebe、Spotless crake等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: Snowy albatross
nodes.summary: Snowy albatross（Snowy albatross，学名 Diomedea exulans），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联8个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括污染。

nodes.name: 凱庫拉
nodes.summary: 凱庫拉是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Snowy albatross，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 新西兰
nodes.summary: 新西兰是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联27种鸟类，包括白眶几维、澳洲潜鸭、太平洋黑鴨等27种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 海鳥
nodes.summary: 海鳥是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联17种鸟类，包括白眼潜鸭、环颈潜鸭、大賊鷗等17种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 爱德华王子群岛
nodes.summary: 爱德华王子群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联2种鸟类，包括Snowy albatross、Great-winged petrel，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联211种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等211种鸟类。

links.evidence: 2008"/> | range_map = Diomedea exulans map.svg | synonyms = Diomedea chionoptera }} The snowy albatross (Diomedea exulans), also known as the wandering albatross, white-winged albatross, or goonie, is a large seabird from the family Diomedeidae; they have a circumpolar range in the Southern Ocean

links.evidence: 2008"/> | range_map = Diomedea exulans map.svg | synonyms = Diomedea chionoptera }} The snowy albatross (Diomedea exulans), also known as the wandering albatross, white-winged albatross, or goonie, is a large seabird from the family Diomedeidae; they have a circumpolar range in the Southern Ocean

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The snowy albatross breeds on South Georgia Island, Crozet Islands, Kerguelen Islands, Prince Edward Islands, and Macquarie Island, is seen feeding year-round off the Kaikōura Peninsula on the east coast of the South Island of New Zealand, and ranges in all the southern oceans from 28° to 60°. Some individual snowy albatrosses are known to circumnavigate the Southern Ocean three times, covering more than , in one year. Snowy albatrosses spend most of their life in flight, landing only to breed and feed

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Snowy albatross at South Georgia Island#South Georgia|South Georgia Island The IUCN lists the snowy albatross as vulnerable status. Adult mortality is 5% to 7.8% per year as of 2003. It has an occurrence range of , although its breeding range is only . The biggest threat to their survival is that they are attracted by the bait for longline fishing and get caught in the same way as the fish; however, pollution is also taking a toll, mainly from plastics and abandoned fishing hooks
